title: Research basis: local models in VM worktrees, parallel local+cloud swarms, per-worktree state recovery, local->cloud escalation, operator Jira board, Flight-Recorder capture, calendar
operator_directive: Operator 2026-05-31: this is the original WP-KERNEL-004 day-1 goal (NOT scope creep). Research GitHub field implementations before building; real code, runtime + fail-scenario tested, adversarially reviewed by sub-agents, hardened. Correctness/robustness/security/state-recovery over speed. No mockups, no planning-only, no paperwork-as-progress.
method: Two parallel general-purpose research agents inspected real GitHub source (Firecracker, cloud-hypervisor, CRIU, Temporal, Restate, LiteLLM, vLLM, OpenHands, Vibe Kanban, Inngest, Prefect, tokio-cron-scheduler, icalendar) and mapped each pattern onto Handshake's existing Rust/Tokio + Tauri code. First-hand verification of the top security finding by the KB.
headline: The VM-worktree/swarm architecture is ~80% already shipped in Handshake; the build is wiring + targeted additions, NOT greenfield. One confirmed CRITICAL security gap (snapshot-clone uniqueness).

existing_infra_verified.sandbox_adapter: src/backend/handshake_core/src/sandbox/adapter.rs: SandboxAdapter has spawn/exec/fs_bind/net_policy/kill/status/exit_code + snapshot/restore/copy_in/copy_out (default Unsupported); IsolationTier {Tier1Container,Tier2Syscall,Tier3Microvm}; TrustClass->min_isolation_tier (UntrustedAgent->Tier3Microvm); AdapterCapabilities.supports_snapshot + gpu_passthrough.
existing_infra_verified.cloud_hypervisor: sandbox/cloud_hypervisor/adapter.rs: REAL snapshot() (ch-remote pause->snapshot file:// producing config/state/memory + verify + resume-on-fail rollback) and restore() into a fresh independent VM (copies snapshot to per-restore scratch, rewrites serial.file, restores from COPY so original stays re-restorable).
existing_infra_verified.swarm_orchestration: swarm_orchestration/: coordinator.rs (single spawn path, registry HashMap<ModelInstanceId,SessionHandle>, two-level fan-out bound = Semaphore + monotonic lifetime ceiling, ClaimLease+TTL+reap_expired reaper, max_respawns_per_instance, budget-as-data, factory-owns-START/coordinator-owns-STOP teardown contract), breaker.rs (FailureFingerprintBreaker Closed->Open->Half-Open), routing.rs (RoutingPolicy::route, TaskTier{Local,Cloud}, LocalOutcome{Failed,LowConfidence}->cloud escalation, per-provider breaker fallback), factory.rs (ModelSessionFactory->LiveSession+SessionTeardown), state.rs (ModelSessionState machine Queued/Loading/Ready/Generating/Completed/Failed/Cancelled with validated transition()), events.rs (SwarmEvent + self-contained SwarmFrEventId table avoiding the .GOV-locked FrEventId).
existing_infra_verified.process_ledger: process_ledger/: Postgres ProcessOwnershipLedger + reclaim.rs (staleness reaper), restart_resume.rs (resumable-state reattach + idempotency + DB-backoff), idempotency.rs, ledger_decorator.rs (wraps every adapter spawn into a START row).
existing_infra_verified.session_checkpoint: session_checkpoint/: event-sourced replay/checkpoint (SessionCheckpoint, EventLedgerRow, IdempotencyLedger, ReplayError).
existing_infra_verified.flight_recorder: flight_recorder/: append-only event ledger (fr_event_registry.rs .GOV-locked + CI alignment test vs FR_EVENT_REGISTRY.json, fr_emitter.rs, span_repo.rs OTel-style spans, duckdb.rs queryable store).

field_alignment: Handshake's day-1 design IS the 2026 field consensus: Cloud Hypervisor as the GPU-capable + snapshot-capable Tier3 microVM (Fly.io CPU-Firecracker/GPU-CloudHypervisor precedent); trust->isolation-tier ladder (containers-are-not-a-boundary); two-layer recovery = VM snapshot/restore (Firecracker/CH pause->create->load{resume_vm}) PLUS event-sourced journal+idempotency (Temporal/Restate durable execution); signature-keyed circuit breaker + per-provider fallback (LiteLLM cooldown); multi-limit admission + per-VM cgroup + rate limiters + lease reaper (Firecracker NSDI'20). Closest single reference: BloopAI/vibe-kanban (Rust+axum+React, git-worktree-per-attempt, execution_process lifecycle rows, broadcast->patch->stream live board).

prioritized_build_plan.evidence: grep of sandbox/cloud_hypervisor/ for vmgenid|entropy|reseed|uniqueness = EMPTY; restore() (adapter.rs:1167) copies the snapshot and restores with resume=true but never rotates VM-generation identity or re-seeds guest entropy. Restoring one base snapshot into N parallel VM worktrees replicates unique IDs, crypto tokens, RNG seeds, entropy pools — the central field caveat (Firecracker VMGenID + random-for-clones docs). HARDEST workload = many parallel worktrees from a golden snapshot, so this is directly in operator scope.
prioritized_build_plan.fix: On restore(), inject a fresh VM-generation id and/or re-seed guest entropy + mark single-use; add a clone-safety regression test (restore one snapshot into two VMs -> assert their generation ids/entropy differ). Tension with [GLOBAL-PORTABILITY]: isolate snapshot_dir behind a configured runtime root.

prioritized_build_plan.item: Grouping/lineage key (project_id/task_id/swarm_id/worktree_id) on SpawnRequest + SessionHandle + SwarmEvent + metadata_jsonb
prioritized_build_plan.evidence: swarm code has only ModelInstanceId{model_id,instance}; no project/task/swarm/worktree grouping. This one field unlocks (1) per-project/task budgets, (2) the Jira board swimlanes, (3) FR drill-down join key, (4) calendar schedule targeting. Lands in ids.rs + metadata_jsonb with no ledger schema change (promote to typed columns later).

prioritized_build_plan.item: FlightRecorder persistence for swarm/VM events + new SwarmFrEventId variants
prioritized_build_plan.evidence: production FlightRecorderSwarmSink only eprintln!s to stderr (no durable DB write, no UI push). Repoint the emit closure to fr_emitter (DuckDB). Add SwarmFrEventId variants WorktreeCreated/Mounted/Reclaimed/CleanupFailed + ScheduledSpinupFired/TeardownFired (FR-EVT-SWARM-WORKTREE-* / -SCHED-*) in the self-contained sub-registry (lock-safe; folded into master FrEventId in a later governance wave). Mirror the ledger overflow-counter so dropped FR events are observable not silent.

prioritized_build_plan.item: Live UI push: SwarmEventSink -> tokio::broadcast -> Tauri emit('swarm://event'); board columns = ModelSessionState, swimlanes = grouping key; ipc::Channel for token stream
prioritized_build_plan.evidence: SwarmControlRoom polls 1500ms. Add a 2nd production sink (UiBroadcastSwarmSink) -> broadcast -> Tauri app.emit; React listen() updates cards in place; keep poll as snapshot/reconcile fallback (guards Prefect-style dropped-message drift). Vibe Kanban snapshot+ready+live-deltas handshake shape, typed SwarmEvent not RFC-6902.

routing.rs escalates on Failed/LowConfidence but NOT on overload (LiteLLM/vLLM escalate on capacity). Add Overloaded fed by coordinator concurrency-semaphore saturation + in-VM OOM/memory-pressure + latency-SLO breach; carry the session_checkpoint idempotency key across escalation so cloud rerun does not duplicate side effects; ForceLocal must block escalation (data-egress).

prioritized_build_plan.item: Admission additions: separate cold-start semaphore, committed-memory budget (no overcommit) + CH balloon, per-device token-bucket rate_limits in ResourceLimits
prioritized_build_plan.evidence: coordinator bounds run-concurrency but not VM cold-starts (TAP/CNI is the scale wall at high parallelism) nor committed memory (Firecracker warns against blind memory overcommit). Add 3 independent limits + per-VM cgroup + per-device rate limiters (anti-noisy-neighbor).

prioritized_build_plan.item: Calendar: tokio-cron-scheduler -> coordinator.spawn_session/cancel_session; time-boxing = existing lease-TTL+reaper (free); ICS export via icalendar
prioritized_build_plan.evidence: no calendar/scheduling exists. tokio-cron-scheduler (Tokio-native, UTC, Postgres-persistable) fires the SAME spawn/cancel the operator button calls; a time-boxed session is a spawn with lease_ttl=box and no renewal so the existing reaper tears it down with ZERO new teardown code (highest-ROI reuse); icalendar generates VEVENTs for CalDAV/Google/Apple import without a calendar server; startup catch-up policy for missed fires on a closed desktop app.

rejected_options: Firecracker as the GPU model-in-VM adapter (no GPU passthrough, roadmap only) -> Cloud Hypervisor is the GPU Tier3 path.
gVisor as primary GPU path (no hardware boundary, cannot snapshot) -> keep as Tier2 option for trusted/reviewed work.
CRIU for in-guest process C/R as primary (CH whole-VM snapshot already captures model+KV-cache live; CRIU GPU C/R fragile) -> future option only.

Adopt LiteLLM / Temporal / Ray / AutoGen / CrewAI as dependencies or external engines (Python proxies / heavyweight distributed schedulers / Go cluster beside a desktop app) -> reuse the PATTERNS in-process Rust, not the runtimes.
SSE/raw WebSocket server inside the Tauri app -> Tauri IPC event+channel IS the transport (Vibe Kanban needed WS only because it is a browser app).
3rd-party event-sourcing crate (cqrs-es etc.) -> FlightRecorder already IS the event store.
Full CalDAV server / OS-level cron -> too heavy / non-portable -> ICS export + tokio-cron-scheduler in-process.
Blind memory overcommit / shared cgroup across worktrees -> admission + balloon + per-VM cgroup.
key_risks: VFIO GPU passthrough weakens the VM boundary (GPU DMA to host) -> gate GPU to Reviewed/Trusted sha256-pinned artifacts; untrusted-agent GPU denied or cloud-routed.
Snapshot-clone identity replication (rank-1) -> VMGenID/entropy reseed + single-use restore.
Absolute snapshot paths tension with [GLOBAL-PORTABILITY] -> isolate snapshot_dir behind configured runtime root.
Escalation cost/egress storm -> bound via lifetime ceiling + per-provider breaker + escalation-rate counter; ForceLocal blocks egress.
Dropped/lagged UI broadcast -> full-snapshot reconcile on broadcast-lag; monotonic seq + single forwarder for ordering.
Missed scheduled fire on closed desktop app -> startup catch-up policy.
FR write failure stalling teardown -> infallible sink trait + out-of-band DuckDB error absorption + overflow counter.
validation_strategy: Each item ships with crash-injection / fail-scenario tests per the operator mandate: clone-safety regression (two restores differ), OOM containment (only the offending VM dies), admission refusal (typed, not host thrash), escalate-on-failure + ForceLocal-blocks-egress, mass-reclaim after host kill, time-boxed lease auto-reap, scheduled-fire FR event emission, board-card-moves-on-pushed-event (WebDriver visual where a display is available).
next_action: Implement rank-1 (snapshot-clone uniqueness, CRITICAL security, headless-provable) FIRST with adversarial sub-agent review, then rank-2 grouping key (structural unlock), then proceed down the plan. GUI-dependent final smokes (rank-4 visual) flagged for a display session.

implementation_progress.consolidated_checkpoint: swarm_orchestration 59/59 + cloud_hypervisor 13/13 lib tests; app-crate build exit 0 (cumulative). feat branch.

rank_1_snapshot_clone_uniqueness.note: Refuses a second concurrently-live restore of one snapshot (CH resume cannot reseed guest identity). Reservation under the gate lock closes the TOCTOU; released on every failure path. Known documented limit: keys on snapshot_dir (copy-bypass needs guest VMGenID, CH does not expose). Adversarially reviewed.

rank_2_grouping_key.note: SpawnRequest swarm_id/worktree_id + builders; SessionHandle carries them; coordinator session_grouping() read API; reaper STOP metadata_jsonb records them. Tested.

commits: 8bff1e07 DurableSwarmFrBridge (sync sink -> async recorder + dropped counter)
rank_3_flight_recorder.remaining: App wiring: construct DurableSwarmFrBridge in SwarmRuntimeState with the shared app-data-root DuckDB recorder (behind duckdb-flight-recorder feature) so deployed events persist. Mechanism tested in default CI via a fake recorder.
rank_3_flight_recorder.note: Durable bridge (8bff1e07) + FR ids (bbdf4cdf). App wiring: production_with_fr_recorder builds DurableSwarmFrBridge from a DuckDB recorder; lib.rs builds swarm_flight_recorder.duckdb under app_data_root and wires it (stderr fallback). Build verifying.

rank_5_overload_escalation.note: LocalOutcome::Overloaded escalates to cloud without tripping the local breaker; ForceLocal blocks egress. Tested (escalate / no-breaker-trip / ForceLocal-blocks).

commits: 16e83da3 live board: Rust forwarder + snapshot command + React CSS-grid board
rank_4_live_ui_push.note: Operator-emphasized Jira board. Backend: SwarmEvent Serialize; production() fans the coordinator sink to FR + BroadcastSwarmSink; spawn_swarm_board_forwarder (single serializer -> ordered typed swarm://event deltas, swarm://resync on lag); kernel_swarm_board_snapshot (cards with state + grouping). Frontend: useSwarmBoard drift-safe hook (snapshot + apply deltas + seq-gap/resync reconcile, 10s safety net not 1500ms poll) + hand-rolled CSS-grid board (7 state columns x grouping swimlanes, per-card cancel, live counts, resync banner). app build exit 0; tsc exit 0. Display-dependent: visual/WebDriver smoke, card drill-down panel, dnd-kit action zones.

rank_6_admission.remaining: Committed-memory admission budget (no overcommit) + CH balloon, per-device token-bucket rate_limits in ResourceLimits. Cold-start bound DONE + tested (8 spawns, run-concurrency 16 + cold-start 2 -> peak boots <=2, all admitted not rejected).

rank_7_calendar.note: SwarmScheduler (cron -> injectable fire callback -> coordinator.spawn time-boxed / cancel), schedules_to_ics (RFC-5545 export). Tested: ICS VEVENT + cron fires within 2.5s. Follow-up: app wiring (construct scheduler, wire callback to coordinator, persist + startup catch-up, ICS export command).

implementation_progress.summary: Ranks 1-7 all implemented + tested + committed on feat. Backend cores done; rank-4 board (Rust+React) and rank-7 calendar (cron+ICS) fully built; rank-3 app FR-recorder wiring building. Remaining: rank-3 app wiring build-confirm; per-adapter rate-limit argv; calendar app wiring; GUI items MT-019/020/027/098 (display); display-dependent board visual smoke.
implementation_progress.board_research: GitHub-surveyed (vibe-kanban, claude-squad, OpenHands, Temporal UI, Inngest, Prefect; dnd-kit/react-virtual). Brief: snapshot+live-delta handshake, typed app.emit deltas + seq/epoch reconcile, hand-rolled CSS-grid (no heavy kanban lib), board-as-read-model (UI writes are commands). Applied in rank-4.
